dailyRoutine.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Daily Routine
dailyRoutine.subtitle: A clean daily loop helps players keep leveling, farming, and unlocking systems without wasting limited combat time.
dailyRoutine.intro: In Ragnarok Origin Classic, the fastest progress comes from doing the right things in the right order instead of grinding randomly. A good daily route usually starts with free rewards and story progress, then shifts into daily commissions, combat-time farming, bosses, and guild content.

items.name: Claim daily rewards first
items.details: Collect daily rewards, event rewards, and battle pass freebies before starting long sessions.
items.why_it_matters: This is the easiest source of early tickets, consumables, and progression materials.

items.name: Push the main story until level-gated
items.details: Main quests remain the main source of early EXP, Job EXP, feature unlocks, and basic gear progression.
items.why_it_matters: Story progression unlocks systems faster than random field grinding.

items.name: Finish daily commissions
items.details: Daily commissions are quick repeatable quests that usually ask players to defeat monsters or complete small objectives.
items.why_it_matters: They provide steady EXP, Zeny, and useful materials while fitting naturally into the daily loop.

items.name: Spend combat time efficiently
items.details: Use combat time on monsters you can kill quickly, ideally targets near your level or mobs tied to needed drops.
items.why_it_matters: Combat time is one of the core efficiency systems for leveling, farming, and drop generation.

items.name: Do daily activities and rotating event tasks
items.details: Regular daily activities and limited events add extra growth materials, bonus rewards, and seasonal progression.
items.why_it_matters: These activities stack account value over time and are often the easiest way to keep up as F2P.

items.name: Hunt Mini and MVP bosses when possible
items.details: Mini and MVP boss content is tied to valuable drops such as rare materials, cards, and blueprints.
items.why_it_matters: Boss content is one of the main ways to convert routine play into long-term gear progression.

items.name: Join guild activities
items.details: Guild quests and guild events add contribution rewards and extra account growth on top of solo play.
items.why_it_matters: Guild participation is a repeatable source of progression that many new players skip too often.

items.name: Use the Handbook before free farming
items.details: The Handbook helps players check upgrade materials, monster locations, and better farming targets.
items.why_it_matters: It prevents wasted time when players hit story walls and need to pivot into efficient farming.

highlights: Daily Commissions, combat time, and rewards should stay at the top of the loop.
Guild tasks and boss hunts add value that compounds over time.
The Handbook is the fastest way to recover when story progress slows down.

skillBuilds.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Skill Builds
skillBuilds.subtitle: Skill-build traffic is highly practical because players usually search after choosing a class and wanting a clear direction.
skillBuilds.intro: Ragnarok Origin Classic build discussions online are already converging around recognizable skill packages rather than one universal point-spread for every player. The most stable homepage format is to show each advanced class with its main skill route, what the route is for, and the core tradeoff between farming, bossing, PvP, and budget friendliness.

items.best_for: Early to midgame farming, steady ranged DPS, general PvE
items.notes: Online build coverage for Sniper consistently highlights ADL and Falcon-oriented setups as one of the most common ranged DPS routes.
Trapper is commonly presented as the alternative to ADL for players who prefer setup damage instead of constant auto attacks.
Current build coverage repeatedly separates Lord Knight between Pierce, Clashing Spiral, Bash, and Crit-based directions.

items.best_for: AoE grinding, party damage, screen-wide control
items.notes: High Wizard discussions consistently frame the class around choosing between its main AoE spell identities.

items.best_for: Tanky frontline play with flexible PvE and PvP options
items.notes: Paladin is one of the clearest examples of a class with multiple viable skill identities instead of a single default path.

items.best_for: Magic DPS players who want either consistent casting or more experimental proc-based gameplay
items.notes: Professor build coverage currently focuses on three recognizable routes: Autocast, Psychic Wave, and SP-focused variants.

items.best_for: Players who want a Merchant branch with strong offensive identity
items.notes: Biochemist build videos consistently compare Acid Demonstration and Hellplant as the two headline directions.

items.best_for: Party support, safer leveling, utility-heavy progression
items.notes: High Priest coverage commonly splits between traditional full-support play and more damage-oriented PvE variants.

highlights: Ragnarok Origin Classic build coverage is strongest when each class route gets a clear role label.
Sniper, Lord Knight, Wizard, and Priest are especially easy for players to compare at a glance.
Showing the skill route, role, and tradeoff together makes the section more useful than a simple list.

classBuilds.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Class Builds
classBuilds.subtitle: Players do not only search for skill trees. They also want a full build direction that combines role, farming comfort, power curve, and stat emphasis.
classBuilds.intro: The most useful homepage version of class builds is not an overlong theorycraft wall. It is a fast overview that tells players what each class archetype actually feels like, what kind of content it suits, and why someone would pick it over a competing job path. For Ragnarok Origin Classic, the strongest publicly documented archetypes already revolve around ranged farming, front-line melee burst, magic AoE, hybrid tank builds, and specialized late-job branches.

items.focus: Attack speed, auto-attack uptime, Falcon synergy
Trap setup, positional play, area denial
strengths: Useful for players who prefer setup gameplay

items.focus: Single-target burst, weapon scaling, aggressive boss pressure
Spell burst, area coverage, magic scaling

strengths: Can play tanky without feeling passive
Good for players who like survivability

items.focus: Magic scaling, proc interactions, specialized resource-based damage
best_for: Players who want flexible mage builds

items.focus: Skill-driven ranged damage with class-specific utility
best_for: Players who want Merchant progression with real damage presence
strengths: Good branch for players tired of basic support expectations

items.focus: Buffs, healing, sustain, optional PvE damage direction
strengths: Flexible enough to branch into more active PvE setups

highlights: A clean class-build section should tell players the role, the feel, and the payoff quickly.
The strongest ROOC archetypes are already easy to group by farming, bossing, PvP, and support value.
This section stays useful even when players are only comparing two or three favorite jobs.

gearProgression.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Gear Progression
gearProgression.subtitle: Players often get stuck once raw leveling slows down and they are no longer sure what to refine, buy, or farm next.
gearProgression.intro: Ragnarok Origin Classic character power is built through several linked systems rather than a single equipment screen. Public beginner and progression guides already point to a consistent growth order: refine core gear efficiently, fill feather slots early, use pets to improve combat output, add cards for damage and utility, and spend shop currencies on long-term value instead of random upgrades.

items.details: Current progression guidance recommends starting with the weapon and core accessories before spreading resources too thin.
items.why_now: This is the most efficient early power jump.

items.details: After the first weapon and accessory upgrades, round out the rest of the equipment so the character is not overinvested in only one slot.
items.why_now: Balanced refine value improves overall consistency.

items.details: Once a stable +6 baseline is set, weapon and accessories become the main refine targets again.
items.why_now: The next meaningful damage spike usually comes from higher refine thresholds.

items.details: Advanced refine effects and stronger scaling become more relevant at higher thresholds.
items.why_now: This is one of the clearest long-term investment tracks for account strength.

items.details: Feathers provide major stat layers such as Attack, Defense, Damage Boost, and Damage Reduction.
items.why_now: A complete feather setup matters more early than over-upgrading one weak feather.

items.focus: Do not overinvest in low-tier feathers
items.details: Set effects are determined by the lowest-rarity feather equipped, so early efficiency comes from coverage first, deep investment later.
items.why_now: This avoids wasting resources on temporary pieces.

items.details: Pet growth in Classic is simplified around level and tier, and pets are positioned as a major help for early and midgame farming.
items.why_now: Pets add practical combat value without requiring a fully finished gear setup first.

items.focus: Prioritize damage and utility cards first
items.details: Cards are sourced from systems such as Monster Annihilation and shop exchange, and are commonly treated as an efficiency layer for farming and combat performance.
items.why_now: Cards improve build identity and help speed up routine content.

items.details: Gold Shop priority starts with feather selection boxes and refine materials; Social Shop prioritizes feathers, then gold, then Blue Star Coin; Pet Shop prioritizes Epic and Legendary fragments; Card Shop specifically highlights Tanuki Card as an early priority.
items.why_now: Correct shop spending keeps progression clean for F2P and light spenders.

highlights: Ragnarok Origin Classic gear growth works best when refine, feathers, pets, cards, and shops all move together.
Early efficiency comes from complete coverage, not from overinvesting in a single slot too soon.
Long-term value decisions matter more than chasing small short-term upgrades.

universeMode.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Zeny Farming
universeMode.subtitle: Best Early and Midgame Currency Routes
universeMode.intro: Currency flow in Ragnarok Origin Classic is tied to efficient daily play rather than one single grind spot. The strongest pattern shown across current guides is to clear one-time treasure value first, then rotate into fast-kill farming, boss content, and disciplined selling so your account keeps growing without wasting combat time.
highlights: Open map treasure chests across different areas before investing heavily into early upgrades.
Spend combat time on monsters you can clear quickly instead of forcing hard targets.
Keep selling unused loot, cards, potions, and food so inventory value keeps moving.

items.how_to_do_it: Open map treasure chests across different areas before investing heavily into early upgrades.
items.why_it_works: A current chest route guide highlights roughly 8 million Zeny available from free chest collection.
items.watch_out_for: Do not treat chest income as your permanent economy plan after the first sweep.

items.how_to_do_it: Spend combat time on monsters you can clear quickly instead of forcing hard targets.
items.best_for: Hunter, Wizard, other efficient ranged or AoE builds
items.why_it_works: Current beginner coverage repeatedly recommends fast kills for better EXP and drop efficiency, which also improves sellable loot flow.
items.watch_out_for: Grinding on slow or elite targets reduces both income and leveling pace.

items.how_to_do_it: Regularly clean inventory and sell extra loot, cards, potions, and food you are not actively using.
items.why_it_works: Current Ragnarok Origin Classic beginner material explicitly points players toward selling unused items for steady income.
items.watch_out_for: Do not dump progression materials you still need for refine, cards, or class growth.

items.how_to_do_it: Join boss runs for rare materials, blueprints, and cards, especially when your class or party can secure stable contribution.
items.best_for: Party players, Biochemist, burst DPS, coordinated guild groups
items.why_it_works: Boss guides describe MVP content as a source of rare items, cards, and blueprints that are not part of normal field farming.
items.watch_out_for: Do not chase every spawn blindly; prioritize bosses that match your build or current gear needs.

items.how_to_do_it: Build your daily loop around monster kills, dungeons, and farming activities that feed the Gold Coin economy.
items.why_it_works: Recent Ragnarok Origin Classic coverage states Gold Coins are earned through gameplay activities rather than premium-only systems.
items.watch_out_for: Avoid spending Gold Coins too early on low-value upgrades when core power systems still need priority.

items.how_to_do_it: Use Merchant class strengths for economy-focused play, crafting support, and smarter market-facing decisions.
items.why_it_works: Current beginner guidance and official class promotion material both frame Merchant paths as economy-oriented classes.
items.watch_out_for: Merchant routes are strongest when paired with planning, not random spending.

communityCreations.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic F2P Guide
communityCreations.subtitle: Safe Start Plan for Free and Low-Spend Players
communityCreations.intro: Ragnarok Origin Classic is currently being positioned as a more F2P-friendly version of the broader Ragnarok Origin ecosystem, with gameplay-earned Gold Coins and fewer direct pay-to-win pressure points. The strongest F2P plan is not about rushing every system at once; it is about choosing a stable class, prioritizing efficient shops, and delaying wasteful upgrades until your account has real direction.
highlights: Pick a stable starter class before spending resources.
Use the main story and Handbook to keep progression efficient.
Delay risky upgrades until your account has real direction.

items.label: Pick a stable starter class
items.details: For beginner comfort, current guides consistently recommend Archer or Mage for safer farming, while Acolyte remains a strong support route. For dedicated F2P value, one recent class guide places Blacksmith at the top of its F2P rankings because of versatility and resource efficiency.

items.label: Push main quests before random grinding
items.details: Use the main story as your early progression spine. It unlocks features, gives steady EXP and job EXP, and prevents wasted time on low-efficiency grinding before your account systems are open.

items.label: Use the Handbook before you farm
items.details: The Handbook is one of the most useful early tools because it shows upgrade materials, monster locations, and EXP information. This lets F2P players target the right monsters instead of burning time and combat stamina inefficiently.

items.label: Prioritize shops that improve account efficiency
items.details: Current shop guidance recommends Feather selection boxes first in the Gold Shop, Feathers then Gold then Blue Star Coin in the Social Shop, Tanuki Card as an early Card Shop priority, and Epic or Legendary pet fragments as long-term Pet Shop targets.

items.label: Refine in controlled checkpoints
items.details: A common efficient path is Weapon +6, then Accessories +6, then full set +6, later Weapon +10 and Accessories +10, and only then pushing further. This avoids the classic F2P trap of overcommitting to risky upgrades too early.

items.label: Fill systems before overinvesting in them
items.details: Feathers, cards, pets, and core gear all scale better when your account has baseline coverage first. Recent guides explicitly recommend filling feather slots early and avoiding heavy investment into weak early-tier pieces.

items.label: Treat spending as acceleration, not access
items.details: Current coverage describes the game as largely accessible through daily content and dungeons, with extra purchases acting mainly as progression speed-ups. One guide also notes that Monthly Pass access can be tied to in-game Gold, which is a major advantage for patient F2P planning.

theIsland.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic MVP Guide
theIsland.subtitle: How MVP Hunting Works and What To Prioritize
theIsland.intro: MVP content is one of the clearest progression breakpoints in Ragnarok Origin Classic because it connects class building, party play, and rare item targeting. The current public guides show that good MVP play is not only about dealing damage; contribution, role balance, daily reward limits, and target selection all affect how much value you get from each hunt.
highlights: Contribution is broader than pure DPS.
Daily reward limits make planning important.
Party play beats brute force in the long run.

items.title: Use the MVP menu before the spawn
items.summary: The MVP interface shows location and countdown, and the game announces spawns shortly before they appear.
highlights: Check spawn time from the Event menu
A system announcement appears 5 minutes before spawn
The interface also shows the party that recently secured the MVP

items.title: Know the two reward layers
items.summary: MVP rewards are split between party-based rewards and individual rewards, so even non-top damage players can still gain value.
highlights: Team Rewards are tied to the MVP title winner inside the party
Team Rewards can include Diamonds, exchange vouchers, blueprint chances, and MVP card chances
Personal Rewards can include accessory blueprints, Elunium, and Headwear Crystal if you participate correctly

items.title: Contribution is broader than pure DPS
items.summary: The scoring system rewards tanking, DPS, healing, and key timing moments instead of only burst damage.
highlights: Damage taken contributes to score
DPS contributes the largest score share
Healing, first strike, final blow, and death penalty all affect results

items.title: Daily attempts matter
items.summary: MVP hunting should be planned because team rewards have a daily limit.
highlights: Current ROO guide material lists a daily limit of 5 MVP team rewards
During MVP Fever the limit rises to 7
Overcapped party members reduce overall score efficiency

items.title: Prioritize bosses that fit your build
items.summary: The strongest route is to chase bosses that drop cards or equipment that directly improve your class.
highlights: High Wizard example: target bosses tied to MATK and cooldown-friendly accessories
Lord Knight example: prioritize bosses linked to physical attack and STR-oriented rings
Do not waste attempts on random MVPs with no direct value to your build

items.title: Party play beats brute force
items.summary: Current beginner material describes smaller bosses as sometimes soloable, but bigger MVPs are clearly better with coordinated teams.
highlights: Bosses are a major source of rare items, cards, and blueprints
Larger MVPs are best handled in parties
Biochemist is currently drawing attention as a strong MVP-focused damage option in community videos

myfaction.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic War of Emperium Guide
myfaction.subtitle: War of Emperium Basics, Roles, and Rewards
myfaction.intro: War of Emperium is one of the most recognizable large-scale guild modes in the Ragnarok formula, and it remains a major reason players invest in guild progression. Current Ragnarok Origin guide material shows that success depends on preparation windows, class role fit, and understanding whether your guild is attacking, defending, or playing for long-term castle value rather than short-term kills.
highlights: Preparation windows matter as much as the fight itself.
Castle ownership unlocks extra guild value.
Role fit matters more than solo hero play.

content: War of Emperium is a guild siege mode where guilds fight to capture and defend castles.
Attackers become defenders by breaking the Emperium inside the castle.
When the timer ends, the guild holding the castle becomes the new occupant.

items.section: When It Happens and Who Can Join
content: Current ROO guide material lists WoE on Sunday at 21:30.
Guild eligibility is checked on Sunday at 5:00, followed by a preparation window from 5:00 to 20:00.
Participation is limited to guilds above Level 3, characters at Base Level 80 or higher, and members who have been in the guild for at least 72 hours.
Priest and Wizard are currently highlighted as S-tier defense picks in one recent class guide.
Priest is valued for healing and sustain during choke-point defense.
Wizard is valued for AoE damage and crowd control at entrances and objective zones.
Blacksmith and Hunter are presented as strong A-tier alternatives for defensive lineups.
Assassin and Knight are currently highlighted as S-tier offensive picks in one recent guide.
Assassin brings burst pressure for fast eliminations and target disruption.
Knight offers durable front-line pressure and consistent offensive presence.
Wizard and Blacksmith remain useful attack-side support options.
Eligible members can complete WoE preparation quests during the preparation window.
The mode includes a WoE item shop and a reward exchange shop tied to participation points.
Joining with preplanned roles matters more than entering as a loose group of individual duelists.
Castle-holding guilds gain access to special rights including a Treasure Chest in the lord's room.
Occupying guilds can also access a Guild Dungeon.
The Guild Dungeon can spawn Mini and MVP targets and offers chances at blueprints and cards.

pcRequirements.subtitle: Join early, pick a role, and keep your weekly guild value high.

minimum.os: Join a guild as soon as the main systems open
minimum.memory: Show up for daily tasks and donation loops
minimum.graphics: Prepare for WoE and party coordination
minimum.storage: Keep time for guild chat and support

recommended.os: Join a guild that matches your play schedule
recommended.processor: Bring a defined PvE or PvP role
recommended.memory: Stack weekly rewards and guild shop value
recommended.storage: Stay active for War of Emperium nights

dlcUnlockables.subtitle: Choose pets that support leveling, farming, and long-term account value.

editionPacks.name: Beginner Pet Picks
includes: Pick one reliable starter pet first
Favor simple damage or sustain bonuses
Avoid niche picks until your core is stable

editionPacks.name: Long-Term Pet Growth
includes: Match pet effects to your class

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Ragnarok Origin Classic

questions.question: What should I do first in Ragnarok Origin Classic?
questions.answer: In Ragnarok Origin Classic, push the main quest first, take your first job at level 10, craft your class weapon at level 15, and start Daily Commissions at level 16.

questions.question: Which class is easiest in Ragnarok Origin Classic?
questions.answer: Ragnarok Origin Classic is easiest for most players on Archer or Mage if they want safe ranged farming, while Acolyte is the smoothest support path.

questions.question: How do I level fast in Ragnarok Origin Classic?
questions.answer: Ragnarok Origin Classic leveling is fastest when you stay quest-first, finish Daily Commissions, use combat time on efficient mobs, and avoid early elite detours.

questions.question: Where do I find Ragnarok Origin Classic codes?
questions.answer: Ragnarok Origin Classic codes are usually shared through official launch pages, Discord, and community posts. Keep the Codes module open and copy the current active entry.

questions.question: Is Ragnarok Origin Classic good for free-to-play players?
questions.answer: Yes. Ragnarok Origin Classic supports a steady F2P route if you focus on daily rewards, efficient gear upgrades, and value-first systems instead of cosmetic spending.

questions.question: What should I join first in Ragnarok Origin Classic: guild or PvP?
questions.answer: In Ragnarok Origin Classic, join a guild early, then branch into PvP, MVP hunting, and War of Emperium once your basic build is stable.

cta.title: Ready to explore Ragnarok Origin Classic?
